Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A soil specimen is tested in a triaxial cell. The soil is first consolidated under a cell pressure of 700 kN/m2 with a back pressure of 350 kN/m2. With drainage prevented, the cell pressure is increased to 800 kN/m2, which causes the pore pressure to rise to 445 kN/m2. An axial load is
then applied, producing a deviator stress of 575 kN/m2 while the cell pressure remains at 800 kN/m2. The pore pressure then increases to 640 kN/m2. The correct pair of values for the pore pressure coefficients A and B respectively is :

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A soil sample is saturated with kerosene oil and its saturated density is 2.32 g/cc. If the specific gravity of soil solids is 2.65 and that of kerosene oil is 0.89, then the void ratio and dry density of the soil respectively are:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A square pile, 400 mm on each side and 10 m long, is driven into a deep uniform clay deposit. If the ultimate load capacity of the pile is 450 kN, the unconfined compressive strength of soil (in kN/m2; round off to two decimal places) is ______ . (Assume α = 0.8 and NC = 9).

NAT
Correct answer: 63.20
Solution

63.20 (62.80 - 63.80)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A clay stratum 8 m thick with void ratio as 1.43 is sandwiched between two sand layers. The permeability of the soil is 3.5 × 10–7 cm/sec and coefficient of compressibility is 0.003 m2/kN. If the time factor for 50% consolidation is 0.196, then the time (in days) required for 50% consolidation of this layer is _____ . (Answer upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 125.64
Solution

125.64 (125 - 126)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A square pile group consists of 25 piles, each of diameter 300 mm and center-to-center spacing as 750 mm is installed in a sandy soil. The group settlement ratio for the pile group is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

In a laboratory permeability experiment on a soil sample, the water level in the standpipe falls from 48 cm to 47 cm in 3 minutes. The time required (in minutes) for the water level to further fall from 47 cm to 38 cm is_____. (Answer upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 30.28
Solution

30.28 (30.1 - 30.4)

Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A 1.2 m thick soil layer is subjected to an upward seepage head of 1.5 m. To ensure a factor of safety of 1.6 against piping, a protective layer of coarse sand (Gs = 2.65, porosity = 0.38) is placed above the soil. The minimum thickness (in meters, round off to three decimal places) of this
protective layer should be______.

NAT
Correct answer: 1.144
Solution

1.144 (1.1 - 1.2)

Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

For the construction of an embankment, the soil is required to be compacted to a maximum dry density of 18.5 kN/m3 at an optimum moisture content of 16%. The available soil in the borrow pit has a natural dry density of 16.8 kN/m3 and a water content of 9%. The volume of the soil (in m3, round off to three decimal places) that needs to be excavated from the borrow pit to provide one cubic meter of compacted embankment is____. [Take Gs = 2.65, γw = 9.81 kN/m3]

NAT
Correct answer: 1.102
Solution

1.102 (1.05 - 1.15)

 
Q18 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A line load of intensity 120 kN/m acts on the ground surface. Using Boussinesq’s theory, the vertical pressure at a point P located at a depth of 4 m was found to be 3 kN/m2. The horizontal distance of point P from the line load is____.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q19 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A square footing of size 2.5 m × 2.5 m is located at a depth of 2 m below the ground surface in a silty-clayey soil. The soil has shear strength parameters as C = 20 kPa and = 20°. The unit weight of soil above and below the water table are 18 kN/m3 and 20 kN/m3 respectively. Assuming general shear failure with a factor of safety of 3, the percentage change in ultimate bearing capacity
if the water table, initially at a large depth, rises to the bottom of the foundation will be____.

 
 
 
 
(All options upto one decimal place)
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
 
Q20 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

At a construction site thickness of a normally consolidated clay layer after primary consolidation is 3.74 m. The initial void ratio of the clay is 0.95 and the compression index is 0.30. The average effective stress acting on the clay layer before construction is 150 kN/m2 and due to the foundation load, the stress on the clay layer increases to 400 kN/m2. The secondary compression index of the clay is 0.025. If the time required for the completion of primary consolidation settlement is two years, then determine the total settlement of the clay layer four years after the completion of primary consolidation. If initial thickness was 4 m.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q21 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

At a laboratory, a pycnometer test is performed at constant temperature using kerosene as the wetting liquid. The following observations were recorded:
• Mass of oven-dry soil = 0.400 kg
• Mass of pycnometer + kerosene filled = 1.599 kg
• Mass of pycnometer + dry soil + kerosene filled = 1.876 kg
If the specific gravity of kerosene is 0.82, then the specific gravity of soil solids is ____. (Answer upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 2.67
Solution

2.67 (2.66 - 2.68)

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33

In an unconfined compression test, a cylindrical specimen of saturated clay 120 mm long and 40 mm in diameter fails under an axial load of 200 N at 15% axial strain. Considering the effect of change in the cross-sectional area of the specimen during loading, the shear strength of the clay will be

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q24 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A 9 m thick layer of saturated clay with saturated unit weight 18.52 kN/m3 overlies a permeable sand stratum. The sand is under an artesian pressure equivalent to 3 m of water head. An open cut excavation is proposed in the clay from the ground surface. Assuming that uplift failure governs the condition, the safe depth of excavation will be: (Take G = 2.67, = 9.81 kN/m3)

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

NAT
Correct answer: 58.54
Solution

58.54 (58 - 59)

In a Proctor compaction test, a soil specimen has a bulk density of 18 kN/m3 and a moisture content of 15%. The specific gravity of the soil solids is 2.65. The moisture content required (in %, round off to two decimal places) to fully saturate the specimen is ______ .

(Take = 9.81 kN/m3).

NAT
Correct answer: 24.94
Solution

24.94 (24 - 26)

Q30 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A standard Newmark’s influence chart is shown in the figure. If the regions labelled A and B are loaded equally, then the resulting pressures at the center will be such that:

 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Newmark’s influence chart is constructed so that each small sector has the same influence at the center.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0

The groundwater table is located at the sand-clay interface, but due to capillary rise, the sand remains saturated upto 3 m above this interface. The saturated unit weights of sand and clay are 20 kN/m3 and 17 kN/m3 respectively and bulk unit weight of sand is 18 kN/m3.
For above data, select the correct statements:

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b)

 
Q32 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A square pile group consisting of 16 piles, each of diameter 0.4 m and length 12 m, is installed to
support a total load of 3000 kN from mat foundation. The piles are spaced at 1.2 m centre to
centre. The foundation soil consists of two distinct clay layers:
• Thickness of soft clay (top) = 6 m
• Thickness of stiff clay (below) = 12 m
• Rock strata is present below the stiff clay.
The water table is at ground level. Soil properties are as follows:
• Saturated unit weight = 18.82 kN/m3, Cc = 0.27, eo = 0.795
Assuming the load distribution ratio as 2 V : 1 H, the probable consolidation settlement (in cm, round off upto one decimal place) of the pile group will be _____ .

NAT
Correct answer: 16.4
Solution

16.4 (16 - 17)

 
 
Q33 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

The FOS against stability is ________ (Correct upto two decimal places).
 
NAT
Correct answer: 2.049
Solution

2.049 (2 - 2.1)
